mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals contraction in manufacturing activity, which foreshadows weakening aggregate demand and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where INDPRO declines month-over-month over a 3-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate above 0.25% signals the start of a monetary tightening cycle that raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below -0.03 or positive returns occur in more than 50% of trigger instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6% and retail sales decline year-over-year simultaneously, it indicates a severe contraction in consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction signal yields positive forward returns in more than 40% of triggered instances over the walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: During the post-GFC zero interest rate period, an increase in housing starts signals strengthening residential investment and consumer confidence, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mean forward return in months with rising HOUST is not significantly greater than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.10) over the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand that drives corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS 3-month change and subsequent 60-day SPX return is less than 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decisive drop in the unemployment rate below 7.0% signals a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The average forward return following trigger events is not statistically significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over the walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation rises rapidly (CPI MoM > 0.3%) while housing starts fall below 900,000 units, it signals a stagflationary environment where rising costs and weakening housing activity jointly depress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive returns in more than 35% of triggered instances, or the Sharpe ratio of the triggered strategy is non-negative over the test period.

mechanism_narrative: During the early expansion phase following a recession trough, robust payroll growth (PAYEMS) confirms that the recovery is strengthening, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors quickly price in improving labor market conditions.
regime_hint: early expansion phase (GDPC1 QoQ > 0 after recession trough)
failure_condition: The mean 5-day return when PAYEMS increases is not significantly different from the unconditional mean in the early expansion regime (two-sample t-test p > 0.10).
